zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Hansard於大約1439年出生在英國林可爾史爾素斯 凱爾西時，他的父親Sir Richard II Hansard46歲，母親Joan Hedworth51歲。他和Thomasine DeBrewse至少有3個兒子。他去世在英國林可爾史爾盧德波洛。

nameMeanings.name: Hansard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): of Norman origin, from the Middle English and Old French personal name Ansard with prosthetic H-; from ancient Germanic ans- ‘(pagan) god’ + ward ‘guard, protector’ or hard- ‘brave, hardy’.

variants.name: Hazard
meanings: English: Anglo-Norman French variant of   Hansard  .

English and French: nickname from Old French, Middle English hasard, hazard ‘game of dice’, perhaps given to a player at hazard, i.e. a gambler, trickster, or rascal. The word probably derives from Arabic az-zahr, from az, assimilated form of the definite article al, + zahr ‘die’. It appears to have been picked up in the Holy Land and brought back to Europe by Provençal crusaders.
English: possibly in some instances of Flemish origin, a nickname from Middle Dutch hasart ‘hare’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Hansard was born about 1439, in South Kelsey, Lincolnshire, England, his father, Sir Richard II Hansard, was 46 and his mother, Joan Hedworth, was 51. He had at least 3 sons with Thomasine DeBrewse. He died in Ludborough, Lincolnshire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Hansard(이)가 대략 1439년 영국 링코실르 소우스 켈시에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Richard II Hansard(은)는 46세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Hedworth(은)는 51세였다. 그는 Thomasine DeBrewse(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 영국 링코실르 루드버루그h에서 사망했다.
